# Tropo Tool Catalog

*Auto-generated 2026-06-19 from vault entries with `type: tool` + `extraction_scope: ship`. Hand-authored content lives in each entry's `trigger_description:` field. Do not edit this file directly — re-run the generator instead.*

## How to invoke

Tools are **typed callables**. The Invocation block under each entry is the executable command. Default working directory is the Studio root (the directory containing `vault/` + `.tropo/`). Exit code 0 = pass; non-zero = drift or failure (each entry's Failure Modes section in the underlying vault entry — linked by UID — documents the specific exit-code contract).

**30 tools** registered in this Studio.

---

## archive() — flip entry state active↔archived with provenance + event

**Domain.** Lifecycle — the canonical gesture for archiving governed vault entries (Option-A state discipline, 99e52c18)

**When to reach for it.** Flip entry state active↔archived with provenance and event.

**Invocation.**
```
python3 vault/tools/6cc9dcdb.py <uid> --reason <reason> [--superseded-by <uid>] [--unarchive] [--force-with-reason <reason>] [--actor <label>]
```

**Implementation.** [vault/tools/6cc9dcdb.py](../vault/tools/6cc9dcdb.py)

**UID.** [`6cc9dcdb`](../vault/files/6cc9dcdb.md) | governed by [`tool.capsule v1.4`](capsules/tool.capsule.md)

## build-release — Tool

**Domain.** Build a Tropo-OS release from the Argo vault — versioned folder, manifest, index, ship artifacts.

**When to reach for it.** Reach for this when executing Stream I (build + ship) of any dev-pipeline cycle. Run with --bump patch (e.g., v1.13.x → v1.14.0 isn't patch — that's feature; v1.14.0 → v1.14.1 IS patch), --bump feature (v1.14.0 → v1.15.0), or --bump release (v1.14.0 → v2.0.0). Step 0 + Step 0b pre-flight gates run validators automatically and refuse to build on validator failures. Use --dry-run to preview the build without writing. Use --force only on retries after fixing the conflict that triggered the overwrite guard.

**Invocation.**
```
python3 vault/tools/a1b8c2d4.py --bump <patch|feature|release> [--dry-run] [--force]
```

**Implementation.** [vault/tools/a1b8c2d4.py](../vault/tools/a1b8c2d4.py)

**UID.** [`a1b8c2d4`](../vault/files/a1b8c2d4.md) | governed by [`tool.capsule v1.4`](capsules/tool.capsule.md)

---

## generate-capability-catalogs — Tool

**Domain.** Generate three Tropo capability catalogs (tool / skill / sa-agent) from vault entries — v1.15 substrate.

**When to reach for it.** Reach for this any time the three Tropo capability catalogs need to be regenerated — typically after adding/amending vault entries with type:tool/how-to/session-agent (new capabilities, edited trigger_description, status changes that affect catalog membership). Also re-run as part of build-release.py preflight to ensure shipped Studios get fresh catalogs. Run with --apply to write; without --apply for dry-run preview that surfaces missing-trigger-description warnings without modifying files.

**Invocation.**
```
python3 vault/tools/d4e9a2c7.py [--apply] [--vault-path PATH]
```

**Implementation.** [vault/tools/d4e9a2c7.py](../vault/tools/d4e9a2c7.py)

**UID.** [`d4e9a2c7`](../vault/files/d4e9a2c7.md) | governed by [`tool.capsule v1.4`](capsules/tool.capsule.md)

## generate-relations-header — Tool

**Domain.** Render Relations + Members navigation blocks from a file's frontmatter — UID-graph navigation surface.

**When to reach for it.** Reach for this when you need to refresh the Relations + Members navigation blocks in a vault entry's body — typically after editing the file's member_of frontmatter or any other UID-reference field. Run --write to modify in place; without --write for dry-run preview. Or run rebuild-vault.py wrapper to refresh Relations + Members across the whole vault as part of the comprehensive cadence (this script is Step 4 of that flow). The Members section uses typed-cap format (5 per type + overflow indicator) so hub entries with many children (tropo-governance has 41 decisions, 175 documents, 72 projects) stay scannable.

**Invocation.**
```
python3 vault/tools/b8e4f1a3.py [--write] [--vault-root PATH] <target>...
```

**Implementation.** [vault/tools/b8e4f1a3.py](../vault/tools/b8e4f1a3.py)

**UID.** [`b8e4f1a3`](../vault/files/b8e4f1a3.md) | governed by [`tool.capsule v1.4`](capsules/tool.capsule.md)

## import-walker.py — Substrate-Internal Walker

**Domain.** Substrate-internal walker for the import primitive (v1.25.0 + v1.26.0 + v1.28.0). Authors sidecars + folder markers + folder mirrors + vault projections; computes hashes via the stable-id → content-aware → sha256 fallback chain; appends to the reconciler journal.

**When to reach for it.** Reach for this when the import primitive needs substrate-internal operations — scanning a Studio for governable folders/files, authoring a sidecar + folder mirror + projection for one source file, running drift detection, applying a reconciler-event payload, or promoting a folder Tier 1 → Tier 2. Most users do NOT invoke this directly; sa.reconciler spawns it for reconciliation, and the v1.25.0 + v1.28.0 onboarding flows trigger create-sidecar implicitly. Direct invocation is for substrate-repair and CI-style scripted import. v1.28.0 v1.0.3 amendments: create-sidecar now co-writes folder-marker MIRROR at vault/files/<folder-uid>.md alongside the on-disk marker (ordered-write protocol with atomic-rename + inline index sync); populates `original_styles:` on .docx projections via shared office_styles library; supports retro-fill for pre-v1.28.0 imports; rebuilds folder mirror Members section from registry walk on every invocation.

**Invocation.**
```
python3 vault/tools/bf886f30.py {scan|create-sidecar|reconcile|apply|promote-folder|extract} [...]
```

**Implementation.** [vault/tools/bf886f30.py](../vault/tools/bf886f30.py)

**UID.** [`bf886f30`](../vault/files/bf886f30.md) | governed by [`tool.capsule v1.4`](capsules/tool.capsule.md)

---

## rebuild-index — Tool

**Domain.** Fast index + project-tree rebuild — vault/00-index.jsonl + 00-project-tree.jsonl from frontmatter (v1.15.1).

**When to reach for it.** Reach for this any time you've added/removed/amended a vault entry's frontmatter and need the index + project-tree to reflect it. Fast (~1-2s on 1460+ entries) and idempotent — safe to re-run frequently. Use this for the common-case edit-and-index cycle; for the comprehensive refresh (index + symlink rehydrate + cascade cleanup) use rebuild-vault.py instead. Run with --apply to write; without --apply for dry-run preview. Reflection-of-frontmatter means new conventions (era, voice_authority, category, audience, etc.) reach the index automatically without script updates.

**Invocation.**
```
python3 vault/tools/f4b8a6e2.py [--apply] [--only UID] [--vault-path PATH]
```

**Implementation.** [vault/tools/f4b8a6e2.py](../vault/tools/f4b8a6e2.py)

**UID.** [`f4b8a6e2`](../vault/files/f4b8a6e2.md) | governed by [`tool.capsule v1.4`](capsules/tool.capsule.md)

---

## rebuild-project-index — Tool

**Domain.** Rebuild a project's 00-index.md from folder contents — 4-column table from frontmatter.

**When to reach for it.** Reach for this when you encounter a projects/<slug>/00-index.md that is missing, stale, or out of sync with the folder contents — typically while navigating to a project, registering a new file in it, or inspecting its status. Idempotent and safe to re-run; produces identical output on unchanged folders. Future v1.5+ may add watcher-triggered automation; today it is reader-triggered (the agent that notices the drift fixes it).

**Invocation.**
```
python3 vault/tools/c6e2b9d4.py --slug <project-slug> [--dry-run]
```

**Implementation.** [vault/tools/c6e2b9d4.py](../vault/tools/c6e2b9d4.py)

**UID.** [`c6e2b9d4`](../vault/files/c6e2b9d4.md) | governed by [`tool.capsule v1.4`](capsules/tool.capsule.md)

## register-kernel — Tool

**Domain.** Register .tropo/ kernel files in the vault index — type: kernel entries pointing at .tropo/ paths.

**When to reach for it.** Reach for this when kernel files have been added/removed/renamed under .tropo/ and the vault index needs to reflect that change. Companion to rebuild-vault.py — that one rebuilds entries authored at vault/files/<uid>.md; this one registers files that stay at their .tropo/ path. Run after kernel directory changes; not part of routine index rebuild. Future v1.16+ may merge these two into a single rebuild script per the Kernel Migration to Vault thesis (1fa26624).

**Invocation.**
```
python3 vault/tools/b3f9d7e2.py [--dry-run]
```

**Implementation.** [vault/tools/b3f9d7e2.py](../vault/tools/b3f9d7e2.py)

**UID.** [`b3f9d7e2`](../vault/files/b3f9d7e2.md) | governed by [`tool.capsule v1.4`](capsules/tool.capsule.md)

## rehydrate — Tool

**Domain.** Rehydrate vault into navigable folder trees — symlinks under 00-tropo-nav/ for active/archived/all views.

**When to reach for it.** Reach for this when the navigable folder tree at 00-tropo-nav/ is stale or missing — typically after running rebuild-vault.py to update the index + project-tree. The symlink hierarchy is the human-navigable surface; rebuild-vault.py rebuilds the data; rehydrate.py rebuilds the navigation. Run rehydrate.py whenever you want the project hierarchy in your editor's file tree to reflect current vault state. The active/archived/all views let you collapse archived noise without losing it.

**Invocation.**
```
python3 vault/tools/a7c5e3f1.py [--output-dir-name DIR] [--vault-path PATH]
```

**Implementation.** [vault/tools/a7c5e3f1.py](../vault/tools/a7c5e3f1.py)

**UID.** [`a7c5e3f1`](../vault/files/a7c5e3f1.md) | governed by [`tool.capsule v1.4`](capsules/tool.capsule.md)

## tropo-backfill-styles

**Domain.** Backfill v1.28.0 substrate fields onto pre-v1.28.0 entries: original_styles: on external-artifact projections + missing folder-marker mirrors. Closes the migration gap for pre-existing imports.

**When to reach for it.** Reach for this when migrating pre-v1.28.0 substrate up to v1.28.0+ shape. THREE modes: (a) `--projection <uid>` backfills original_styles: on a single external-artifact entry. (b) `--all --yes` sweeps all external-artifact entries without original_styles:; populates them by opening the source binary + calling office_styles.extract_office_styles(). (c) `--folder-markers --yes` (v0.5 NEW) sweeps on-disk .tropo-folder.md markers without matching vault mirrors at vault/files/<marker-uid>.md; co-authors the missing mirror using retro-fill semantics per arch-spec §3.5.5 Amendment 1 v0.5. Sweep modes require --yes to bypass TTY prompt; bare sweep on non-TTY refuses (per spec §3.13 v0.5 precondition 4). Idempotent unless --force. Mike

**Invocation.**
```
python3 vault/tools/09ef9843.py (--projection <uid> | --all | --folder-markers) [--yes] [--force]
```

**Implementation.** [vault/tools/09ef9843.py](../vault/tools/09ef9843.py)

**UID.** [`09ef9843`](../vault/files/09ef9843.md) | governed by [`tool.capsule v1.4`](capsules/tool.capsule.md)

---

## tropo-export

**Domain.** Export an agent-edited markdown working-copy back to a .docx deliverable. The deliverable side of the v1.28.0 import → work → export loop. Two paths: preservation (source binary as scaffold; max fidelity) vs transformation (registered template as scaffold).

**When to reach for it.** Reach for this when a user wants to ship an agent-edited working-copy back as a .docx deliverable. Default invocation (no --template flag) = PRESERVATION export: open the working-copy

**Invocation.**
```
python3 vault/tools/ce9dbcc2.py --working-copy <uid> [--template <uid>] [--output-path <path>] [--title <string>] [--accept-binary-drift]
```

**Implementation.** [vault/tools/ce9dbcc2.py](../vault/tools/ce9dbcc2.py)

**UID.** [`ce9dbcc2`](../vault/files/ce9dbcc2.md) | governed by [`tool.capsule v1.4`](capsules/tool.capsule.md)

## tropo-register-template

**Domain.** Register a user-uploaded Word template as a governed docx-template. The format-reference half of the v1.28.0 export gesture — pairs with tropo-export.py --template <uid> for transformation export.

**When to reach for it.** Reach for this when a user wants to register a Word template for transformation-export use. The user supplies the .docx path + a lowercase-hyphenated slug ([a-z0-9-]+). Tool copies the binary to .tropo-studio/templates/<slug>.docx, extracts style metadata via the shared office_styles library, and authors a governed docx-template entry. Slug-collision discrimination: 

**Invocation.**
```
python3 vault/tools/1056eec5.py --path <path-to-docx> --name <slug> [--description "..."] [--use-case "..."] [--force]
```

**Implementation.** [vault/tools/1056eec5.py](../vault/tools/1056eec5.py)

**UID.** [`1056eec5`](../vault/files/1056eec5.md) | governed by [`tool.capsule v1.4`](capsules/tool.capsule.md)

## validate-no-absolute-paths — Tool

**Domain.** Vault portability validator — fails if any committed file contains absolute machine paths (/Users/.../, ~/...).

**When to reach for it.** Reach for this whenever verifying vault portability — that no committed file has absolute machine paths leaking in (typical contamination: log artifacts, cached output, agent output that captured a /Users/<account>/ prefix). Run as part of build-release.py pre-flight (the build is supposed to ship a portable artifact). Also useful before commits that touched generated content. The vault

**Invocation.**
```
python3 vault/tools/c9b7d4e2.py [--strict] [vault-root]
```

**UID.** [`c9b7d4e2`](../vault/files/c9b7d4e2.md) | governed by [`tool.capsule v1.4`](capsules/tool.capsule.md)

## validate-release-manifest — Tool

**Domain.** Validate the ship-artifact manifest graph — 23 checks per ship-artifact.capsule v1.1.4.

**When to reach for it.** Reach for this when verifying the ship-artifact manifest is structurally clean — every ship-artifact entry has valid frontmatter, parent UIDs resolve, output_path is unique, supersession pairs are bidirectional, override-child shape is legal. Run before build-release.py and after any ship-artifact entry edit. Importable shape (`from validate_release_manifest import validate_manifest`) lets build-release.py call it programmatically without subprocess overhead. Pairs with validate-canonical-l0 + validate-capability-membership + tropo-validate as the structural-validator quartet.

**Invocation.**
```
python3 vault/tools/e1d8c3a7.py [--vault-path PATH]
```

**Implementation.** [vault/tools/e1d8c3a7.py](../vault/tools/e1d8c3a7.py)

**UID.** [`e1d8c3a7`](../vault/files/e1d8c3a7.md) | governed by [`tool.capsule v1.4`](capsules/tool.capsule.md)
